hyperedges.label: LTX Model Evolution Lineage (v0.9 -> 13B -> LTX-2 -> LTX-2.3)
IC-LoRA Control Ecosystem (Technique + AVControl + ComfyUI Nodes)
LTX Cloud Deployment Ecosystem (Studio + fal.ai + Replicate + HF Spaces + RunPod)

hyperedges.rationale: These models form the competitive landscape for AI video generation as benchmarked in early 2026, with LTX-2.3 leading open-source and Runway Gen-4.5 leading overall.

LTX Video can be accessed through multiple deployment pathways: ComfyUI nodes for visual workflows, HuggingFace Diffusers for Python scripting, the official REST API for production, and cloud hosting via Fal.ai.

NVIDIA's RTX AI Garage ecosystem integrates Lightricks LTX models, ComfyUI workflows, Blender 3D, and RTX Video Super Resolution into a unified local AI video generation pipeline, showcased at CES and GDC 2026.

These three models are positioned as the leading open-source video generation models competing in 2026, each with different strengths: LTX-2.3 (speed, audio, 4K), HunyuanVideo-1.5 (multi-person scenes), Wan 2.2 (referenced as benchmark competitor). Source comparisons establish this competitive triangle.

Seven distinct cloud platforms host LTX Video models for inference, forming a comprehensive cloud deployment ecosystem. Each offers different pricing models (serverless, GPU rental, managed API) and different feature sets (LoRA training, web UI, REST API).

These components form the core technical architecture of LTX-2/2.3: Video-VAE (1:192 compression) and Audio VAE handle encoding, DiT is the backbone transformer, Gemma 3 12B is the text encoder, rGAN and Video DWT Loss are novel training innovations. Together they define the model's technical identity.

LTX-Video architecture combines Video-VAE (with 1:192 compression, 128 latent channels, 32x32x8 spatial-temporal compression), a denoising decoder that performs final diffusion step in pixel space, and a Video-DiT transformer with RoPE fractional positional encoding - all tightly integrated in a shared compressed latent space.

LTX-2.3 (22B parameters) is the first open-source model generating synchronized audio and video in a single generative pass. Uses Gemma 3 12B for text conditioning, separate Audio/Video VAEs for decoding, MultimodalGuider for independent per-modality CFG/STG/cross-modal guidance control, and LTXVNormalizingSampler to prevent audio clipping artifacts.

The LTX LoRA training ecosystem spans official tools (LTX-Video-Trainer, ltx-trainer package in LTX-2 monorepo) and third-party cloud services (WaveSpeedAI at $3.50/1000 steps, fal.ai, Oxen.ai). Supports LoRA, IC-LoRA, Control LoRA, and full fine-tuning. Training under 1 hour for motion/style/likeness LoRAs. LoRA compatibility breaks between major model versions documented.

hyperedges.relation: LTX-2.3 architecture comprises rebuilt VAE, 4x larger gated attention text connector, improved HiFi-GAN vocoder, and Gemma 3 12B text encoder, totaling 22B parameters
Version 0.9.7 was the biggest upgrade, scaling from 2B to 13B parameters and introducing spatial upscaler, distilled variants, and FP8 quantization

LTX LoRA ecosystem includes style, motion, IC-LoRA (depth/canny/pose), camera control, ID-LoRA (identity), and Union IC-LoRA (combined control) types, with camera LoRAs incompatible with IC-LoRA in same generation
OPTIMIZATION_STRATEGIES enable running LTX-Video on consumer hardware with varying quality-performance tradeoffs
LTX-2 ARCHITECTURE combines dual-stream transformer, audio VAE, modality-aware guidance, thinking tokens, and Gemma 3 encoder for joint audio-video generation
VIDEO_GENERATION_COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE where LTX-2.3 competes against CogVideoX, Wan 2.2, Veo 3, Sora 2, and Ovi across speed, quality, audio, and duration metrics
VAE innovations enable 1:192 compression by relocating patchifier into VAE encoder and using denoising decoder for final-step detail recovery
LTX-2 architecture combines asymmetric dual-stream DiT with Gemma 3-12B text encoder, bimodal CFG, and causal audio VAE to achieve synchronized audio-video generation
LTX Desktop runs LTX-2.3 locally with XML timeline import from DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, and Final Cut Pro, enabling mid-production AI video editing

LTX-Video architecture integrates Video-VAE (1:192 compression) with DiT via holistic approach where denoising is shared between transformer and VAE decoder, using T5-XXL text encoder and rectified flow training

LTX-2 (19B) employs asymmetric dual-stream DiT (14B video + 5B audio) coupled via bidirectional cross-attention, with Gemma 3-12B text encoder enhanced by thinking tokens and modality-aware CFG for independent text/cross-modal guidance

AVControl introduces parallel canvas conditioning on LTX-2 backbone, enabling per-modality LoRA training for 13 control types; adopted by downstream works JUST-DUB-IT (video dubbing) and ID-LoRA (identity personalization)

LTX-Video v0.9.8 (13B, Distilled, Up to 60s Video)

LTX vs Closed Models (Sora, Veo, Runway)
Human Evaluation Benchmarks (85% T2V Win Rate)
